title: ixeris pygmaea
intro: Ixeris pygmaea, also known as dwarf groundsel, is a small annual herb that belongs to the daisy family and is native to East Asia. Traditionally, Ixeris pygmaea has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Ixeris pygmaea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible application in treating various skin conditions and infections. Ixeris pygmaea can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
